failure_modes.text: Outcome overfitting can make the past feel inevitable and hide the actual quality of the original decision.
failure_modes.mitigation: Judge the decision by the quality of the process and evidence at the time, not by the outcome; overfitting to results hides the actual decision logic.

failure_modes.text: WYSIATI can trap the team in the available story instead of searching for the missing branch or missing information.
failure_modes.source_quote: WYSIATI (What You See Is All There Is)
failure_modes.mitigation: Actively search for the missing branch: ask what information was absent or what alternative was never explored, rather than accepting the available story.

failure_modes.text: Counterfactuals become dangerous when they drift into fiction untethered from plausible causal structure and context.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Counterfactuals Become Fiction Instead of Discipline
failure_modes.mitigation: Anchor counterfactuals to plausible causal structure: require that every 'what if' trace a mechanism that could have actually operated.

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe to accept this conclusion?
What other plausible paths deserve attention right now before we lock in this plan?
premortem_questions.source_quote: what other plausible paths deserve attention right now

premortem_questions.text: If this project has already failed spectacularly, what branches or assumptions did we miss?
premortem_questions.source_quote: imagine that the endeavor has failed spectacularly

heuristics.text: Mandate a devil's advocate or dialectic standard so every dominant story meets an explicit antithesis.
Require independent judgments before discussion so the first view does not anchor the full branch set.
Run a pre-mortem before commitment so the team works backward from failure and exposes hidden branches.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on branch recovery, anti-inevitability discipline, and pre-mortem or devil's-advocate practice.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor scenario catalogs because the raw source is stronger on the structural discipline for generating and testing alternatives.
open_questions: The first premortem item normalizes the abbreviation so it reads clearly as a reusable question chunk.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on systems-thinking, hypothesis-driven testing, and design-style iteration for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strong on confirmation-bias and motivated reasoning, but availability-style conflicts remain less clean at the current canonical target surface.
structured_tensions: The source is tension-real on confirmation-bias and anchoring-risk from early discussion order, even without a dedicated structured tension block.
